title: NPM Basics Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of package.json/package-lock.json, common commands (init/install), and the vast package ecosystem for dependency management.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primarily purpose of a 'package.json' file?
options: To serve as a 'manifest' for your project, listing all dependencies, project metadata, and common terminal scripts.
To hide the JavaScript code from the console.

questions.question: Which and/or what was the primarily role of 'package-lock.json' in a project?
options: To automatically update all packages to their newest major version.
To 'pin' the exact version of every single package and sub-package your project uses, ensuring stable builds for your team.

questions.question: Which command would you use to create a new 'package.json' file from the terminal?
Which and/or what command would you use to install a package specifically as a 'DevDependency'?
Does NPM (Node Package Manager) allowing you to build truly Fullstack applications by using millions of pre-built packages and libraries?
options: Yes, by providing a robust ecosystem for managing, sharing, and versioning your code.
